task.summary: Evaluate advisory output for selecting Radeq work-example routes without diluting the complete-website positioning.

model_or_worker.name: Claude CLI and Codex GPT route-strategy agent

reasoning_profile.change_policy: Use as advisory route strategy only; implementation still requires an approved spec and local verification.

token_efficiency_profile.context_scope: Compact route list, current preview, existing demo infrastructure, and mesh constraints.

input_packet_summary.summary: A bounded Radeq route-strategy packet covering the owner's proposed /ukazky routes, current primary website offer, existing demo modules, and constraints.

output_pointer.summary: Accepted synthesis: first-wave examples should prove complete website delivery, e-shop offer clarity, lead/data flow, and handoff; chatbot, design, workflow, and storage system should be deferred or merged.

prompt_or_input_delta.summary: No rerun was needed; Claude and GPT route-strategy outputs converged on the same prioritization pattern.
prompt_or_input_delta.rollback_path: Remove this eval and work-log entry if the owner chooses a different route taxonomy.

verification_evidence.evidence: Current preview returned HTTP 200 and the repo shows existing module IDs for blog/docs, service landing, admin dashboard, and e-shop offers.
Claude and GPT independently recommended a narrow first wave anchored on website delivery and adjacent add-ons rather than seven equal routes.
Decision Mesh constraints preserved public-route caution, model-output evaluation, and project mesh lifecycle handling.
